matrix.requirement: Correct or prove the Release ordering 'load-bearing' claim
matrix.covers: Primary finding: reversed order does not cause double-acquisition (structurally unreachable); it does break eviction progress. Deterministic (no goroutine race needed -- manual sequencing on the same object reproduces the exact window).
matrix.mutation_check: Reversing the real Release order should reproduce this test's own progress-broken assertion; verified manually during self-consistency, then reverted (production ordering is unchanged/kept as-is since it's still the one that lets eviction progress).

matrix.test: n/a (doc-only, reviewed by re-reading corrected guard.go)
matrix.covers: Release's doc comment no longer makes an unproven/false correctness claim; states the real, test-backed behavior.

matrix.requirement: Isolate the !inProgress eviction-gate clause from refs==0
matrix.covers: Secondary finding: directly constructs refs==0/inProgress states independent of TryAcquire's side effects.
matrix.mutation_check: Removing the !inProgress clause from the gate (leaving only refs==0) must make this NEW test fail. Verified during self-consistency.

matrix.requirement: Existing TestFileGuardRegistryRetainsInProgressEntries's doc claim must match what it actually proves
matrix.covers: No longer overclaims 'verifies the eviction gate directly'; reframed as integration-level confirmation.
matrix.mutation_check: Removing !inProgress clause: this test continues to PASS (documented as expected/known, matching verifier's finding) -- not a regression, a deliberately-kept demonstration of why the isolated test above was needed.

matrix.requirement: No regression to existing FileGuard behavior
matrix.covers: TestSplitInProgressCAS, TestAcquireReleaseReacquire, TestIndependentFileIDs, TestReleaseWithoutHoldingIsNoOp, TestFileGuardRegistryBounded, TestInProgressObservability, plus orchestrate_test.go's FileGuard-consuming tests
